focus: Final synthesis: consolidated findings across all 10 research questions (Q1-Q10) with evidence citations; identified registry sync issues

answer: 15 additional bugs/drift identified across core authored docs beyond the 3 known INSTALL_GUIDE issues: version drift, tool count drift, database path drift, packet pointer drift, cross-doc version inconsistencies, weak prose in database path descriptions

8 primary authored docs mapped to sk-doc types and validated for contract compliance: SKILL.md (skill), README.md (readme), INSTALL_GUIDE.md (install_guide), ARCHITECTURE.md (reference), feature_catalog.md (reference), manual_testing_playbook.md (playbook), mcp_server/README.md (readme), mcp_server/handlers/README.md (readme)

feature_catalog index does NOT validate as --type playbook (no feature_catalog document type exists); per-feature files are NOT discoverable via per-type contract; both require manual recursion and follow their own contract

manual_testing_playbook index DOES validate as --type playbook; per-scenario files ARE discoverable via per-type contract as playbook_feature type through path pattern detection; manual recursion still required due to validator limitation

SKILL.md hook first (dependency root for INSTALL_GUIDE version fix), then batch by file-type: Batch A (References HVR pass), Batch B (mcp_server per-folder README usefulness audit), Batch C (INSTALL_GUIDE drift fixes)

3 worst HVR pitfalls: (1) Semicolons in complex explanatory sentences, (2) Oxford commas in lists, (3) Em dashes for emphasis. Use short simple sentences, bullet-point lists, and follow clean prose style from HVR-compliant reference docs
